title: Migrate recognition and album identity contracts to category-neutral identities
summary: Replace car-specific identity fields with a versioned category identity that supports scientific names while preserving album/archive compatibility.
objective: Let the existing application foundation consume Lepidoptera labels without hardcoding one recognition category.
context: The original contract uses make/model fields; the accepted plan requires CategoryIdentity with category ID, stable class ID, scientific name, display name, and optional alternate names.
outputs: Documented invariants, error states, and ownership boundaries
implementation_notes: Represent uncertain and unsupported results explicitly.
Keep platform types, database entities, ONNX tensors, and UI state out of shared domain contracts.
Use stable class IDs rather than display names as identity keys.
acceptance_criteria: Recognition candidates and album entries use CategoryIdentity without car-only required fields.
Existing stored car identities have a documented backward-compatible decoding or migration rule.
The fake engine and contract fixtures include Lepidoptera identities and reject unknown category/class combinations.
Serialization and archive versions change only where required and are documented.
verification: Run shared serialization and contract fixture tests.
Run archive backward-compatibility tests using existing car fixtures.
Validate category/class identity uniqueness and required scientific-name fields.
edge_cases: Manual identity outside the model catalog

title: Accept the licensed Lepidoptera catalog, dataset, and checkpoint candidate
summary: Pin the CC BY 4.0 Figshare dataset record, define the 162-class MVP boundary, and admit the exact MaxViT-T checkpoint only for provenance and mobile qualification.
objective: Provide an auditable category and artifact boundary without downloading the full 315 GiB image collection.
context: The accepted MVP amendment replaces cars with Austrian butterflies and moths while preserving car artifacts for post-MVP work.
outputs: Pinned checkpoint identity and acquisition policy
implementation_notes: Treat dataset images, annotations, pretrained weights, and generated model weights as separately licensed artifacts.
Do not equate public download access with permission to redistribute.
Include generation or approximate-year distinctions only where the accepted product scope requires them.
acceptance_criteria: The catalog contains exactly the source classes with at least 50 expert-validated images.
The dataset record, article, scripts, and checkpoint licenses are treated as distinct artifacts.
The checkpoint file ID, size, source URL, and published MD5 are pinned before download.
Full dataset acquisition remains conditional on checkpoint qualification or approved distillation.
verification: Validate catalog uniqueness and exact source-count threshold.
Re-fetch Figshare metadata and compare immutable artifact fields.
Confirm CC BY 4.0 attribution requirements are represented in model/application notices.
Confirm no raw dataset or model binary is committed.
edge_cases: Multiple names for the same generation
Dataset labels that collapse generations required by the MVP
non_goals: Approving the checkpoint as a release model
Deleting or rewriting post-MVP car assets
handoff_notes: ML work must consume the accepted stable class IDs and license conclusions without renaming classes silently.

title: Select the Android benchmark device and measurement protocol
summary: Choose the representative Android 10+ mid-range device and define a reproducible cold/warm inference measurement protocol.
objective: Give the accepted two-second median inference threshold a concrete hardware and measurement reference.
context: The planning package requires a selected device but intentionally leaves the exact device open.
inputs: Accepted Android 10+, recent mid-range, approximately 4 GB RAM device boundary
Accepted median inference below two seconds signal
outputs: Measurement protocol covering setup, warmup, sample count, timings, and result format
implementation_notes: Record model name, chipset, RAM, Android version, ABI, and relevant power/performance settings.
Separate image decoding/preprocessing, session initialization, and inference timing so results are interpretable.
acceptance_criteria: A physically available or otherwise guaranteed-access benchmark device is named with reproducible specifications.
The protocol defines cold and warm paths, warmup, minimum sample count, percentile/median reporting, and failure handling.
The human owner confirms access to the selected device.
verification: Review the protocol against the project acceptance signal.
Run a no-model dry run or fixture timing harness if device access is already available.
non_goals: Meeting the latency gate before a candidate model exists

title: Set the bundled application and model size budget
summary: Define accepted download, installed, model-file, and runtime-memory budgets for the closed-testing build.
objective: Bound model selection and quantization decisions with explicit release constraints.
context: The merged planning package leaves the bundled-size budget unresolved while requiring a bundled offline model.
inputs: Supported approximately 4 GB RAM device boundary
outputs: Accepted size and memory budget decision record
A measurement checklist for release artifacts and runtime memory
implementation_notes: Keep compressed download size, installed size, ONNX file size, peak resident memory, and inference working memory distinct.
Document which metric is a hard gate and which is an optimization target.
acceptance_criteria: Numeric budgets are recorded for model file, download package, installed footprint, and peak runtime memory.
The measurement method and responsible evidence location are defined.
The human owner accepts the budgets or explicitly records a blocking exception.
verification: Check the decision record contains every required metric and unit.
Validate the proposed measurement commands or Android tooling on a placeholder build when available.
non_goals: Optimizing a model before a baseline exists
Changing the accepted offline bundled-model requirement

title: Create the reproducible Python ML environment
summary: Set up pinned Python tooling for dataset manifests, PyTorch/TorchVision training, evaluation, ONNX export, and ONNX Runtime verification.
objective: Make ML experiments and export checks reproducible without committing environments, caches, datasets, or checkpoints.
context: The accepted stack is Python with PyTorch/TorchVision and ONNX export; exact dependencies must be pinned after planning.
outputs: Pinned Python dependency definition and lock data
Ignore rules for datasets, caches, runs, and model checkpoints
implementation_notes: Pin Python and key framework/runtime versions explicitly.
Separate runtime, development, and optional accelerator dependencies where practical.
Do not assume CUDA is available; the baseline pipeline must run on CPU for smoke tests.
acceptance_criteria: A clean supported environment can install dependencies using one documented command.
Imports and a minimal tensor-to-ONNX Runtime smoke path succeed.
Generated datasets, caches, checkpoints, and local environments are ignored by Git.
Dependency versions and supported Python version are recorded.
verification: Create a clean environment and run the smoke test.
Run dependency consistency/check tooling.
Use Git status to prove generated local artifacts are not tracked.

title: Create the Kotlin Multiplatform Android project foundation
summary: Create a buildable Kotlin Multiplatform and Compose Multiplatform project with Android as the only shipped MVP target and clear shared/platform module boundaries.
objective: Establish the smallest reproducible application skeleton that later tasks can extend without changing the accepted architecture.
context: The repository currently contains lifecycle artifacts but no application implementation. The accepted plan assigns shared domain/UI code and Android platform adapters to separate areas.
outputs: Buildable shared and Android application modules
Minimal Android launcher and Compose root
Developer setup documentation and baseline CI-ready commands
implementation_notes: Configure Android minimum SDK to match Android 10/API 29 unless a documented toolchain constraint requires a compatible equivalent.
Do not add iOS or desktop deliverables; shared code should avoid unnecessary Android coupling.
Keep dependency versions centralized and do not add network, auth, analytics, or cloud SDKs.
acceptance_criteria: A clean checkout can invoke the documented Gradle wrapper commands without manual file edits.
The Android debug application assembles and launches to a minimal Compose root.
Shared domain code compiles without importing Android UI, filesystem, database, or ONNX APIs.
Static checks and unit-test source sets are wired for later tasks.
verification: Run the Gradle wrapper version command.
Run the Android debug assemble task.
Run configured shared unit tests and static checks.
Inspect the dependency graph for unapproved network or telemetry libraries.
edge_cases: Offline Gradle execution after dependencies are cached
handoff_notes: All application tasks use the documented wrapper commands and extend these module boundaries.

title: Define the versioned album archive contract
summary: Specify a checksummed, privacy-preserving export/import archive with validation, conflict rules, and atomic failure semantics.
objective: Allow local backup and transfer without coupling the durable format to internal database details.
context: Manual export/import is an accepted MVP requirement and must be suitable for later migration or synchronization.
implementation_notes: Use logical entry IDs and relative archive paths; never persist device-absolute file paths.
Validate all metadata, paths, sizes, and checksums before mutating local state.
Exclude unnecessary original image metadata from exported managed copies.
acceptance_criteria: The contract contains archive version, timestamps, entries, relative image references, and checksums.
Path traversal, missing files, checksum mismatch, incompatible versions, duplicates, and partial archives have defined outcomes.
Import validation is separable from commit so invalid archives cause no partial mutation.
The format does not expose internal database implementation details.
verification: Validate good, corrupted, missing-file, duplicate-ID, incompatible-version, and path-traversal fixtures.
Review fixtures for absence of location and unnecessary device metadata.

title: Define the versioned model bundle manifest
summary: Specify and validate the model manifest, class map, preprocessing, output interpretation, hashes, ONNX compatibility, and license references.
objective: Make the training export and Android runtime consume one deterministic model contract.
context: Cross-runtime accuracy depends on exact agreement about input transforms, class order, and output ranking.
implementation_notes: Include model and class-map hashes, input tensor name/shape/type, color order, resize/crop, normalization, output tensor semantics, opset, runtime minimums, and notices.
Do not commit a real model binary as part of this contract task.
acceptance_criteria: A validator rejects missing, duplicate, reordered, or hash-inconsistent class metadata.
Preprocessing and postprocessing are unambiguous enough for independent Python and Kotlin implementations.
License and attribution references are mandatory fields for release-ready manifests.
Manifest version compatibility behavior is documented.
verification: Validate positive and negative manifest fixtures.
Round-trip the example manifest through Kotlin and Python JSON tooling when those harnesses exist.
Review the contract against ONNX Runtime input/output metadata needs.
non_goals: Training, quantizing, or benchmarking the model

title: Implement local album persistence behind the shared repository
summary: Implement transactional Android persistence for album metadata and private app-managed image references behind AlbumRepository.
objective: Provide restart-safe local album storage without leaking database or Android types into shared domain code.
context: The MVP is local-only and album entries must survive restarts while supporting edits, deletes, search, and favorite filtering.
outputs: Persistence unit and Android integration tests
implementation_notes: Store only managed relative image identifiers or validated private paths, never transient gallery URIs as durable references.
Define deterministic search behavior and sort order.
Keep database transactions explicit for multi-record operations.
acceptance_criteria: Create, observe, update, favorite, annotate, search/filter, and delete operations satisfy the shared contract.
Entries survive process recreation and application restart.
Database details do not escape the adapter boundary.
Deleting an entry has a documented and tested managed-image cleanup policy.
verification: Run repository contract tests against the real adapter.
Run migration/schema tests.
Run Android restart persistence tests.
Inspect stored records for absence of original external URIs and unnecessary metadata.

title: Implement Android image acquisition and sanitization adapters
summary: Implement camera and system gallery acquisition, deterministic orientation handling, preprocessing-source preparation, metadata removal, and private managed-image storage.
objective: Turn user-selected images into stable private inputs without retaining unnecessary location or device metadata.
context: Both camera and gallery inputs are required, but only app-managed sanitized copies may back durable album entries.
outputs: Private managed-image store and cleanup behavior
implementation_notes: Prefer system picker/camera contracts that minimize broad storage permissions.
Decode with bounded memory and preserve correct visual orientation before stripping metadata.
Use atomic file creation/rename where possible and clean temporary files on cancel or error.
acceptance_criteria: Camera and gallery flows each produce a readable private app-managed image.
The managed copy renders with correct orientation and contains no unnecessary EXIF location/device metadata.
Canceled, malformed, oversized, and unreadable inputs return recoverable errors without orphaned durable entries.
The app does not rely on permanent access to the original gallery item.
verification: Run instrumentation tests with rotated, metadata-rich, large, malformed, and canceled fixtures.
Inspect managed outputs with an independent metadata reader.
Measure peak decode memory on representative large images.

title: Publish cross-lane contract fixtures and fake adapters
summary: Provide deterministic recognition, album, model-manifest, and archive fixtures plus fake interfaces that unblock application, ML, and verification lanes.
objective: Let parallel consumers implement and test against the same examples before real adapters are complete.
context: The planning package explicitly allows the application lane to use a deterministic fake recognition engine.
implementation_notes: Fixtures must cover successful, uncertain, unsupported, corrupt, and incompatible cases.
Keep fixtures small and free of third-party images or unclear licenses.
acceptance_criteria: Application tests can drive all recognition result states without ONNX Runtime.
Archive and manifest consumers share identical checked-in fixtures.
Fake adapters implement the accepted interfaces without production-only behavior.
Fixture ownership and change-review rules are documented.
verification: Run contract and fake-adapter tests.
Validate every JSON fixture against its schema.
Compile fake implementations with the shared domain module.

title: Materialize the licensed Lepidoptera catalog and deterministic evaluation sample
summary: Build acquisition tooling for Figshare metadata, the 162 eligible classes, and a small deterministic evaluation sample without fetching every species archive.
objective: Create a reproducible and auditable dataset boundary suitable for checkpoint verification and later balanced distillation.
context: The source contains 541,677 images and roughly 315 GiB of archives; checkpoint-first evaluation avoids unnecessary acquisition.
implementation_notes: Do not commit raw images, private URLs, access tokens, or credentials.
Split by original source/vehicle identity or near-duplicate group where possible to avoid leakage.
Record missing, corrupt, ambiguous, and duplicate samples rather than silently dropping them.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly 162 classes satisfy the accepted minimum-count rule.
Acquisition is resumable, hash-checked, and scoped to selected files or samples.
Every acquired artifact records Figshare file ID, URL, size, checksum, license, and attribution.
Raw archives, samples, and credentials remain outside Git.
verification: Re-fetch and validate class counts.
Run catalog/schema consistency tests.
Repeat sample selection and compare hashes.
Confirm generated/raw data is ignored by Git.

title: Build model information and privacy disclosures
summary: Implement an in-app information screen showing offline behavior, supported catalog/version, limitations, license notices, and local-data privacy behavior.
objective: Give casual users transparent expectations and satisfy model/asset notice requirements without adding telemetry or accounts.
context: The plan includes an About Model screen backed by ModelManifest and required license references.
implementation_notes: Read version and catalog data from the bundled manifest instead of duplicating constants.
Avoid promises of exact identification and explain ranked confirmation.
Keep required notices accessible offline.
acceptance_criteria: The displayed model/catalog version matches the bundled manifest.
Required notices and source acknowledgements are available offline.
The screen states that inference and album data remain local and that unsupported/uncertain results may occur.
No telemetry, account, subscription, or remote-service wording is implied.
verification: Run UI tests with valid and missing/invalid manifest fixtures.
Compare rendered version/classes/notices with fixture data.
Review disclosure copy against merged requirements and license obligations.

title: Implement recognition orchestration with the fake engine
summary: Implement shared recognition use cases and presentation state against RecognitionEngine using the deterministic fake adapter until ONNX integration is ready.
objective: Unblock end-to-end application flow while preserving one replaceable inference boundary.
context: The accepted planning package explicitly permits a fake RecognitionEngine, and real inference belongs to the ML lane.
outputs: Unit tests driven by fake responses
implementation_notes: Run recognition away from the UI thread and make concurrent submissions deterministic.
Preserve candidate order, scores, model version, and timing without inventing confidence claims.
Manual correction is album metadata only and never a training submission.
acceptance_criteria: Every contract result state maps to an explicit presentation state.
Duplicate taps, cancellation, retry, and stale completion cannot save the wrong result.
A candidate confirmation or manual correction produces a valid save command but does not persist until explicitly confirmed.
Replacing the fake engine with the production adapter requires no screen or domain contract change.
verification: Run deterministic unit tests for success, uncertain, unsupported, error, cancellation, retry, and out-of-order completion.
Run concurrency tests with controlled fake delays.

title: Implement validated album export and atomic import
summary: Implement versioned, checksummed archive export and two-phase validate-then-commit import behind shared use cases.
objective: Provide reliable manual backup and transfer without partial mutation or internal database coupling.
context: The accepted MVP requires manual export/import, and the archive contract defines compatibility, conflict, privacy, and path-safety rules.
implementation_notes: Validate archive version, paths, declared sizes, checksums, entry references, and resource limits before database/file mutation.
Stage imported files privately and commit metadata/files as one recoverable operation.
Return a human-readable preview and structured errors for UI consumption.
acceptance_criteria: A valid export imports into an empty profile with equivalent album data and image bytes.
Corrupt, incompatible, traversal, missing-file, duplicate, oversized, or partial archives cause no partial durable mutation.
Conflict behavior is deterministic and matches the accepted contract.
Exports contain sanitized managed copies and no internal absolute paths.
verification: Run archive round-trip tests.
Run every positive and negative contract fixture.
Inject failures during staging and commit and verify rollback/cleanup.
Inspect archive contents for path and metadata privacy.
edge_cases: Zip bomb or excessive file count
handoff_notes: The application-experience batch can bind Data Transfer UI directly to the structured preview and result types.

title: Build the album browsing experience
summary: Implement the default album screen with empty, loading, populated, filtered, and recoverable-error states.
objective: Give casual users a clear home for browsing and finding saved Lepidoptera specimen sightings.
context: The accepted MVP requires local entries, favorites, notes, search, and filtering; the album is the default destination.
outputs: Navigation entry points for capture/import and detail
implementation_notes: Keep search/filter state explicit and testable.
Use stable entry IDs for list identity and avoid loading full-resolution images into list cells.
Make empty states distinguish no collection from no filter matches.
acceptance_criteria: The screen renders empty, populated, filtered, and recoverable-error states from deterministic fixtures.
Search and filters update results predictably without mutating stored entries.
Favorite state and edited labels/notes reflect repository updates.
Large collections use bounded image loading and stable list identity.
verification: Run presentation-state unit tests.
Run Compose UI tests for empty, search, filter, and navigation states.
Exercise a synthetic large album and inspect scrolling memory behavior.
proof_required: Screenshots for empty, populated, and filtered states

title: Create independent shared-contract regression suites
summary: Verify domain, model-manifest, archive, and fake-adapter contracts independently from their implementing lane.
objective: Prevent parallel application and ML work from drifting away from accepted shared interfaces.
context: Contract fixtures are the first cross-lane dependency and require independent schema, compatibility, and behavior checks.
implementation_notes: Consume public interfaces and checked-in fixtures rather than implementation internals.
Include failures for duplicate class IDs, hash mismatch, incompatible versions, traversal paths, corrupt archives, and unsupported recognition states.
acceptance_criteria: Every shared contract has at least one positive and one negative independent test.
Manifest, archive, and domain fixtures are validated without relying on producer self-tests.
Compatibility and error outcomes match the accepted policy.
The suite runs through documented repository commands.
verification: Run the independent suite against current contracts.
Intentionally corrupt representative fixtures and confirm failures are detected.
Record covered invariants and any gaps.

title: Inspect and reproduce the pinned MaxViT-T checkpoint
summary: Download the exact Figshare checkpoint, verify hashes and state-dict structure, recover its authoritative class order, and reproduce source-model inference.
objective: Determine whether the published checkpoint is a technically and legally usable starting point before training or distillation.
context: The candidate is 122,783,842 bytes with published MD5 040fea4abf59a631d41e3878f51d073c and reported 97.87% source test accuracy.
outputs: Locally computed SHA-256 and verified MD5
Reproducible PyTorch inference command and metrics
implementation_notes: Record code revision, environment lock hash, dataset/split hashes, seed, transforms, hyperparameters, and weight source for each run.
Use preprocessing that can be expressed exactly in the model bundle contract.
Keep selection metrics separate from the held-out test set.
acceptance_criteria: Downloaded bytes match the pinned size and MD5 and receive a recorded SHA-256.
The architecture and output dimension match the supported class map exactly.
Inference preprocessing is reconstructed from primary source code and documented.
Evaluation distinguishes source-reproduction evidence from independent release claims.
verification: Verify size, MD5, and SHA-256.
Load the checkpoint strictly into the documented architecture.
Run deterministic inference twice on the frozen sample.
Compare observed behavior with published preprocessing and metrics.

title: Build camera and gallery acquisition flows
summary: Connect the Android camera and system gallery adapters to a shared source-selection and acquisition experience.
objective: Let users reliably start recognition from a new photo or an existing image with clear permission and failure handling.
context: Both image sources are required and must feed the same managed-image and recognition pipeline.
outputs: Progress, cancel, permission, and recoverable-error states
implementation_notes: Request only the permissions actually required by the selected system contracts.
Prevent duplicate launches and stale results after process or navigation changes.
Never create an album entry before recognition is explicitly confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: Camera and gallery each reach recognition review with the correct managed image.
Cancel, denied permission, unreadable input, low storage, and process recreation are recoverable.
Repeated taps do not create duplicate acquisitions or recognition jobs.
No broad media permission is requested when the system picker makes it unnecessary.
verification: Run Compose and Android instrumentation tests for both sources.
Test permission denial, cancel, malformed input, and saved-state restoration.
Inspect the manifest and runtime permission requests.
proof_required: Screenshots of chooser and recoverable errors

title: Build ranked recognition review and manual correction
summary: Implement candidate ranking, uncertainty/unsupported messaging, confirmation, retry, cancellation, and manual identity correction before saving.
objective: Make model output transparent and useful without presenting uncertain predictions as facts.
context: The accepted UX shows ranked candidates and permits manual correction when none is right.
outputs: Manual make/model and optional generation/year correction
Explicit confirm/save, retry, and cancel actions
implementation_notes: Do not translate raw scores into misleading percentages unless the model contract defines calibrated confidence.
Preserve manual identities as user-confirmed metadata and label them distinctly where useful.
Guard saving against stale or duplicate confirmation events.
acceptance_criteria: Ordered candidates display the correct identity information and remain selectable.
Uncertain, unsupported, empty, and engine-error states use honest language and offer valid next actions.
Manual correction validates required make/model fields and optional generation/year fields.
Exactly one album entry is created only after explicit confirmation.
verification: Run UI tests for every fake recognition fixture.
Run duplicate-confirmation and stale-result tests.
Manually review wording for unsupported and uncertain states.
proof_required: Screenshots of ranked, uncertain, unsupported, and manual-correction states

title: Build album data transfer experience
summary: Implement export destination selection and import preview/confirmation around the validated archive engine.
objective: Make local backup and transfer understandable while preventing accidental or partial imports.
context: Manual export/import is the only MVP backup and transfer mechanism.
outputs: Import file selection and validation progress
Explicit import confirmation and result reporting
implementation_notes: Use system document APIs without requesting broad filesystem permissions.
Display archive version, entry/image counts, conflicts, and validation errors before confirmation.
Never offer confirmation for an invalid archive.
acceptance_criteria: A valid export can be saved through the system document flow.
A valid import is previewed without mutation and requires explicit confirmation.
Invalid, incompatible, corrupt, canceled, and low-storage cases are clear and recoverable.
The screen reports completion only after the atomic import succeeds.
verification: Run UI/instrumentation tests using all archive contract fixtures.
Test canceled document flows and permission revocation.
Verify no mutation occurs during preview or failed import.
proof_required: Screenshots of valid preview and invalid archive states
non_goals: Cloud drives beyond system document-provider behavior

title: Build album entry detail and editing
summary: Implement entry viewing, favorite toggling, notes editing, identity correction, and confirmed deletion.
objective: Let users maintain their local collection after an entry has been saved.
context: Favorites, personal notes, confirmed identity, date, and deletion are part of the accepted album behavior.
implementation_notes: Make unsaved edits and save failures visible.
Deletion confirmation must explain that the managed photo copy is removed according to repository policy.
Do not expose internal paths or database identifiers.
acceptance_criteria: Saved edits persist across restart and update album search/filter results.
Canceling edits leaves stored data unchanged.
Deletion requires confirmation and returns safely to the album.
Missing images and concurrent deletion produce recoverable states.
verification: Run presentation and UI tests for edit/save/cancel/delete flows.
Run restart persistence verification after editing.
Inject repository failures and verify user-visible recovery.
proof_required: Screenshots of detail, editing, and delete confirmation

title: Independently evaluate the Lepidoptera checkpoint
summary: Evaluate candidate checkpoints with top-one/top-five, per-class, confusion, calibration-oriented, and unsupported-input evidence using frozen splits.
objective: Measure the accepted top-five gate without allowing frequent classes to conceal weak supported classes.
context: Published source metrics are encouraging but do not replace an independently frozen, per-class evaluation with explicit imbalance and unsupported-input reporting.
outputs: Failure gallery referencing licensed/local fixture IDs rather than copied images
implementation_notes: Report macro and weighted metrics with sample counts.
Keep unsupported/out-of-distribution experiments separate from the supported-class top-five gate.
Never tune on the held-out results without versioning a new selection/test protocol.
acceptance_criteria: Evaluation verifies dataset, split, class-map, code, environment, and checkpoint identities before running.
Top-five accuracy and per-class results are generated for every supported class.
Classes with no or insufficient test support fail validation rather than disappearing from reports.
Reports preserve enough metadata for independent reproduction.
verification: Run evaluation on a known fixture with expected rankings.
Cross-check metric calculations with an independent implementation or library.
Validate the machine-readable report schema and class coverage.
edge_cases: Fewer than five total classes in fixtures

title: Red-team photo privacy and archive integrity
summary: Challenge metadata removal, private storage, archive path handling, checksums, resource limits, conflicts, and atomic import behavior.
objective: Prove that local-only storage and manual transfer do not leak unnecessary metadata or corrupt the album under hostile inputs.
context: Privacy and archive safety are explicit merged boundaries, and invalid imports must cause no partial mutation.
implementation_notes: Inspect actual saved and exported image bytes with an independent metadata reader.
Test traversal, absolute paths, symlinks if applicable, duplicate names/IDs, checksum mismatch, excessive counts/sizes, truncation, and interruption.
Snapshot album/file state before each failed import and compare afterward.
acceptance_criteria: Managed and exported copies contain no unnecessary location/device metadata.
Private files are not exposed through unintended public paths or logs.
All malicious, corrupt, incompatible, and resource-exhaustion fixtures fail before durable mutation.
Injected failures leave album metadata and managed files in a consistent state with temporary data cleaned.
verification: Run independent EXIF/metadata inspection.
Run adversarial archive corpus and resource-limit cases.
Run failure injection at staging and commit boundaries and compare state snapshots.

title: Verify Android application flows and restart persistence
summary: Create independent Android instrumentation coverage for camera/gallery acquisition, ranked review, album behavior, edits, search/filter, transfer, and process restart.
objective: Demonstrate that the accepted user experience works on Android beyond isolated implementation tests.
context: The application batch spans platform adapters, shared state, local persistence, and six user-facing screens.
implementation_notes: Use deterministic test data and fake inference for application-only paths, then repeat a critical path with the production adapter.
Exercise system picker/camera/document boundaries using supported instrumentation techniques without weakening permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Camera and gallery flows each reach explicit confirmation and create exactly one durable entry.
Favorites, notes, identity edits, search/filter, and deletion persist correctly across restart.
Export/import round-trips through the user flow and invalid archives never enable confirmation.
Canceled, denied, malformed, low-storage, and missing-image states are recoverable and do not create orphan records.
verification: Run the suite on an emulator and at least one supported physical device.
Force-stop/relaunch or recreate process state at defined checkpoints.
Inspect runtime permissions and private storage behavior.
proof_required: Screenshots or recording of critical flows

title: Export and verify ONNX model bundles
summary: Export selected PyTorch candidates to ONNX with a matching manifest/class map and verify numeric and ranked-output equivalence in ONNX Runtime.
objective: Produce portable candidate bundles whose runtime behavior matches the evaluated PyTorch model.
context: Export the qualified 162-class MaxViT-T candidate first; if unsupported operators, size, or equivalence fail, hand off to ML-008.
outputs: Generated candidate bundle outside Git until approved
Manifest/class map with hashes and notices
implementation_notes: Use the supported modern PyTorch ONNX export path and pin opset/runtime compatibility.
Compare preprocessing inputs, raw outputs, ordered candidates, and evaluation metrics.
A failed or unsupported export remains a candidate failure and must not be bundled.
acceptance_criteria: The manifest hash, class map, input/output metadata, and exported model agree.
ONNX Runtime loads the model using the pinned environment and required operators.
Numeric differences remain within documented tolerances and ranked candidates meet the equivalence policy on frozen fixtures.
Held-out top-five/per-class metrics are reproduced with the exported model within documented tolerance.
verification: Run export verification from a clean environment.
Run fixture-level numeric/ranking equivalence.
Re-run held-out evaluation through ONNX Runtime.
Validate manifest and model hashes.
non_goals: Approving a release model before size/device gates

title: Implement the Android ONNX Runtime recognition adapter
summary: Implement Android model loading, exact preprocessing, background inference, top-k postprocessing, cancellation, and structured errors behind RecognitionEngine.
objective: Replace the fake engine without changing shared application or UI contracts.
context: Platform-specific inference belongs behind the accepted shared boundary and must preserve the model manifest semantics.
implementation_notes: Load inference off the UI thread and reuse sessions safely.
Validate bundle hashes and metadata before accepting inference requests.
Use bounded image buffers and release native/tensor resources deterministically.
Do not add network fallback or telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: The adapter implements the shared interface without leaking ONNX or Android types.
Android fixture rankings match the verified Python ONNX results within the accepted equivalence policy.
Invalid/mismatched bundles fail safely with explicit errors.
Inference cancellation, repeated calls, and lifecycle recreation do not leak sessions or save stale results.
verification: Run Android fixture equivalence tests.
Run invalid manifest/hash/class-map tests.
Exercise repeated and canceled inference while observing native memory.
Run the app in airplane mode.

title: Optimize and qualify the bundled model on the benchmark device
summary: Measure baseline candidates, apply compatible optimization or quantization, and select a bundle that satisfies accepted accuracy, latency, size, and memory gates.
objective: Produce the release-candidate bundled model with traceable tradeoffs and no silent weakening of acceptance thresholds.
context: Qualify the checkpoint-derived ONNX bundle on the Samsung Galaxy S20 FE before approving any full-data retraining or distillation.
outputs: Model card documenting limitations and selection rationale
implementation_notes: Measure before and after each optimization and reject changes that break class mapping or equivalence.
Use the accepted device protocol and distinguish cold load, warm preprocessing, and inference timings.
Only the approved release candidate enters the bundled model path; large binary storage strategy must follow repository policy.
acceptance_criteria: The selected ONNX bundle retains at least 80% held-out top-five accuracy and complete per-class reporting.
Median inference is below two seconds under the accepted benchmark protocol.
Model, package, installed, and peak-memory measurements satisfy the accepted budgets or record a human-approved blocker.
Cross-runtime equivalence, manifest hashes, license notices, and model card all match the selected artifact.
verification: Reproduce held-out and per-class evaluation after optimization.
Run PyTorch/ONNX and Python/Android ranking equivalence.
Run the accepted cold/warm device benchmark and memory/package measurements.
Validate the final manifest and artifact hashes from a clean build.
proof_required: Final model card and manifest validation
non_goals: Changing the accepted accuracy or device thresholds
handoff_notes: Verification and Release must independently reproduce the final reports before closed testing.

title: Distill a mobile Lepidoptera model when the checkpoint fails device gates
summary: Conditionally acquire a balanced source subset and distill or fine-tune a mobile-suitable classifier only when MaxViT-T fails accepted ONNX, size, memory, or latency gates.
objective: Produce a smaller 162-class model without downloading the full source collection unnecessarily.
context: This fallback is activated by documented ML-005 or ML-007 failure evidence, not by preference.
implementation_notes: Cap abundant classes deterministically while retaining all eligible minority samples.
Keep teacher outputs and source images outside Git.
Do not activate unless checkpoint device qualification fails.
acceptance_criteria: The activation reason references a failed accepted gate.
Subset acquisition is reproducible, licensed, attributed, and leakage-resistant.
The student preserves all 162 outputs and meets held-out/per-class accuracy gates.
Run metadata records teacher, data, environment, seed, transforms, and hashes.
verification: Validate subset manifests and hashes.
Run deterministic fixture training.
Evaluate held-out and per-class metrics.
Hand the candidate back through ONNX equivalence and device qualification.
notes: Blocked until MaxViT-T qualification produces a concrete failing gate.

title: Independently reproduce ML accuracy, equivalence, and device gates
summary: Re-run held-out/per-class evaluation, PyTorch/ONNX/Android ranking equivalence, and the accepted device latency/size/memory protocol for the release candidate.
objective: Ensure model acceptance is based on independently reproducible evidence rather than producer self-report.
context: The accepted model gates are at least 80% held-out top-five accuracy and median inference below two seconds on the selected device, within accepted budgets.
outputs: Independent aggregate and per-class evaluation report
implementation_notes: Verify hashes of code, environment, splits, model, manifest, and class map before running.
Do not tune or replace artifacts during independent verification; failures return to the owning task.
Record thermal/power state and raw timing samples.
acceptance_criteria: Independent held-out top-five accuracy is at least 80% and includes every supported class with sample counts.
Python PyTorch, Python ONNX Runtime, and Android ONNX Runtime rankings satisfy the accepted equivalence policy.
Median device inference is below two seconds under the accepted protocol.
All accepted size and peak-memory budgets pass.
The final gate decision references immutable artifact and input hashes.
verification: Run all evaluation and equivalence commands from a clean environment.
Run the full benchmark protocol on the named physical device.
Cross-check package and installed-size measurements using independent tools.
non_goals: Selecting a different model during verification
Marketing claims beyond the accepted test scope

title: Verify the complete application in airplane mode
summary: Exercise install-to-album, recognition, correction, persistence, search/filter, export, and import with network access disabled.
objective: Prove the product's central subscription-free and remote-service-free promise as a complete user journey.
context: Recognition and collection behavior must work fully offline with a bundled model and local data.
inputs: Representative supported and unsupported image fixtures
implementation_notes: Enable airplane mode before first application launch after installation where practical.
Observe network requests or failures with platform tooling; the app must not require connectivity to proceed.
Include both ranked-candidate confirmation and manual correction.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh installation launches and exposes the bundled model/catalog without network access.
Camera and gallery images can be recognized, confirmed/corrected, and saved offline.
Album edits, restart persistence, search/filter, export, and import work offline.
No recognition, account, telemetry, or remote configuration request is required or retried in a user-disruptive way.
verification: Run the documented journey on a supported physical device in airplane mode.
Inspect system/network logs for unexpected requests.
Restart the device/application and repeat a saved-entry lookup.

title: Publish and verify a Google Play closed-testing build
summary: Prepare signed release configuration and compliance materials, publish to a closed track, and verify install/update behavior with selected testers.
objective: Meet the accepted MVP distribution boundary without expanding into a public production launch.
context: Google Play closed testing is required; public release remains post-MVP. Credentials and approvals remain human-managed.
inputs: Human-managed signing and Play Console access
outputs: Closed-track release checklist and compliance records
Tester install/update evidence and rollback notes
implementation_notes: Never commit keystores, passwords, service credentials, or Play Console secrets.
Pin and record application ID, versioning, artifact hashes, model/catalog version, notices, and release notes.
Stop if store declarations conflict with the actual local-only behavior or required licenses.
acceptance_criteria: All prerequisite verification gates pass and reference the exact release artifact/model hashes.
A signed release artifact satisfying accepted size budgets is uploaded to a Google Play closed-testing track.
At least one authorized tester installs the build through Play and verifies an update or documents why first-release update proof is deferred.
Store disclosures accurately describe local photos/data, offline inference, permissions, and absence of accounts/telemetry.
No secret material is present in Git or logs.
verification: Rebuild the release artifact from a clean checkout using documented secret injection.
Compare uploaded artifact and model hashes with approved evidence.
Perform closed-track install and update checks.
Run a secret scan and review Play declarations/notices.
handoff_notes: After this task is accepted, MVP implementation proof exists; public release still requires a separately approved post-MVP decision.
